filename: Biosafety Manual for cell culture room A227a1.docx

full_text:
Biosafety Manual for cell culture room A227a1 (BSL 1)

Please read this manual carefully before starting any experiments. These rules apply to biosafety level 1 biological agents in room A227a1. However, no set of rules can substitute for careful working procedures, use of common sense and consideration of others.

General rules

Biosafety level 1 (BSL1) organisms used in this room are cell lines and cultured patient tissues. Their use will be contained in the laminars and incubators marked with Biohazard-sign. You will not be allowed to work in this room unless you have received adequate training in safe cell culture techniques.

The cell culture door is kept closed at all times. Sticky mat is put on the floor at the room entrance to reduce the amount of contaminants introduced into the room by researchers.

No eating or drinking is allowed in the laboratory. (Not even chewing gum!)

Before and after the work all the surfaces in use and researcher’s gloves must be sprayed with 70% ethanol for decontamination.

Glass, sharp, plastic and cell culture waste must be disposed according to the Biomedicum Helsinki waste handling rules and procedures, see below.

Gloves, lab coat and if appropriate, mask/safety glasses must be worn at all times.

A record has to be kept on all experiments. This is your own responsibility and required by GTLK (Board of Gene Technology). For each experiment, record date, organisms used, and if the material is to be transferred for storage – location of the storages.

Cell culture plates or trays holding the plates must all contain the following information: Date, your initials and cell line, passage number, culture media type, type of treatment. These plates are to be kept in the CO2 incubator marked with the Biohazard-sign.

Waste disposal

The amount of BSL1-class waste is small, max 20kg/week solid waste and approx. 5L liquid waste/week.

Solid waste

Cell culture and patient material- derived waste is collected into the autoclavable waste bags outside the laminar. When full, this bags are closed and placed into another big autoclavable bag to make a double-bag. These bags are sealed, marked with a barcode and “Polttoon – To be burnt” marking, and taken directly by the user to the central waste room on the P-floor to be autoclaved.

Sharp waste Waste containing knives and needles is to be collected as sharp waste into hard-wall plastic containers. Markings: Sharp waste, barcode or billing details. The container should be marked with red tape/sticker and brought to the corresponding waste container in the central waste room on the P-floor .

Glass waste Pasteur pipettes are to be collected into the plastic container and when container is full – disinfected overnight with 2% P3-Triquart solution, then dried and disposed to the laboratory glass waste containers located in the central waste room on the P-floor. Clean glass waste (reagent bottles, etc.) is disposed to the normal glass waste containers located in the central waste room on the P-floor.

i)- and ii)- wastes are brought to the corresponding waste containers in the central waste room on the P-floor by the researchers, not by the cleaning personnel!

Solid waste which was not in contact with the biological material can be put to the normal mixed or energy waste bins. These bins will be emptied by the cleaning personnel.

Liquid waste When working with clean cells you can aspirate the media into the suction bottle supplied with final concentration of 2% of P3-Triquart disinfection solution. When the suction bottle is full, let the liquid stay overnight and then pour the liquid into the sink. Add new P3-triquart into the bottle before putting the suction system back together.

Spills and Emergencies

Spills in the working area and the laminar have to be decontaminated wiped out immediately with 70% ethanol (provided in spray bottle). Accidents in room A227a1 must be reported immediately to the people in charge of biosafety.

Before leaving the cell culture room

Clean up your things from the laminar. Wipe the laminar and all the other surfaces used with 70% ethanol from the spray bottle. Rinse the suction hose with water and 70% ethanol.

If full, take the waste bags downstairs to the central waste room. Decontaminate Pasteur pipettes waste if container is full, change suction bottle, if full.

Discard your gloves. Wash your hands.

GOOD LUCK WITH YOUR EXPERIMENTS!
